verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 (Cysteine-Rich Secretory Protein 1) is a glycoprotein with a GPI anchor (glycosylphosphatidylinositol anchor) that tethers it to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. It is expressed on the surface of immune cells, particularly T cells and NK cells, where it plays a role in cell-cell adhesion and immune regulation. The GPI anchor is a stable, covalent mechanism for outer-leaflet anchoring that is resistant to detergent extraction and persists after washing. This qualifies CRISP1 as a bona fide surface-accessible protein under its baseline localization.
